methodsAttempted.query: site:psu.edu Artificial Intelligence academic integrity Penn State generative
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the central Academic Integrity AI page plus campus, college, news, and course-specific items.
Followed official AI at Penn State page links to AI Studio and Data Protection pages for concrete data-handling evidence.

methodsAttempted.query: site:researchsupport.psu.edu Penn State generative AI in research
methodsAttempted.notes: Selected Office for Research Protections guidance because it contains concrete research, disclosure, privacy, and human-subjects AI guidance.

artifacts.summary: Firecrawl discovery and fetches found central official Penn State AI guidance, academic integrity, AI Studio, data-protection, and Office for Research Protections pages. Unit-specific, event/news, course-PDF, and lower-specificity library guide sources were recorded as rejections and were not used for university-wide claims.

artifacts.claimText: Penn State guidance says AI tools used by multiple people in a class, program, or administrative office require an accessibility review before use.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Penn State says instructors need department head and dean approval, or appropriate commonwealth campus academic leadership approval, before requesting AI-assisted grading software.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Penn State's Academic Integrity page makes student use of content-generating AI tools course- and assignment-specific and warns that improper use can be an academic integrity violation.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Penn State says academic integrity policies apply to student use or misuse of all tools, including university-approved AI tools.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Penn State's AI Studio user agreement permits Level 1 and Level 2 data in AI Studio and prohibits Level 3 and Level 4 data.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Artificial Intelligence | Academic Integrity
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: In some classes, students may use AI tools. In some classes, students may not use AI tools. And in some classes, students may use AI tools for some, but not all, assignments or tasks. This applies to the use of all content-generating AI tools... The improper use of these tools can be an academic integrity violation.

artifacts.notes: Claim is narrower than the Guidelines page and directly supported by the accessibility-review evidence snippet.
Claim is scoped to the approval-before-request requirement for AI-assisted grading software and does not generalize beyond the source.
Claim preserves the course- and assignment-specific scope of the Academic Integrity page.
Claim is directly supported by the FAQ statement about student use or misuse of all tools.
Claim is restricted to the AI Studio user-agreement permitted/prohibited data language.
Claim combines only the adjacent Data Protection instructions on free personal accounts and Level 3/4 information.
Claim is directly supported by the ORP Core Responsibilities section.
Claim is scoped to identifiable or restricted human-subjects data and the explicit IRB/data-agreement approval condition.

artifacts.title: Pennsylvania State University AI Policy Staged Review
artifacts.summary: Staged evidence for Pennsylvania State University uses central official Penn State AI, Academic Integrity, AI Studio, Data Protection, and Office for Research Protections sources. The claim set covers accessibility review for multi-user AI tools, AI-assisted grading approval, course-specific student AI use, academic-integrity applicability to AI tools, AI Studio data classifications, free personal AI account restrictions for Penn State work, and research/human-subjects generative AI responsibilities.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
AI at Penn State robots.txt returned a website 404 page during a direct robots check; the official pages were fetched by Firecrawl and no disallow rule was observed for the selected page paths.
Rejected official-but-unsuitable sources include unit-scoped, event/news, course-specific PDF, and lower-specificity library guide pages.
Claims are staged candidates only and do not set publishAsCanonical or isCanonical.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
